stack.title: Tech Stack
stack.subtitle: Nirvana's tech stack organized in architectural layers. Click on any technology to explore my real use cases and code examples.

openai.narrative: OpenAI provides powerful APIs for generative AI, including GPT-4 for natural language understanding, DALL-E for image generation, and Whisper for speech recognition. Widely used for building chatbots, content generation, code assistants, and intelligent search systems.

watsonx.narrative: IBM Watsonx is an enterprise AI platform for training, fine-tuning, and deploying machine learning models at scale. Designed for regulated industries requiring on-premise deployment, data governance, and compliance. Supports foundation models like IBM Granite.

ollama.narrative: Ollama enables running large language models locally on your machine. Supports models like Llama 3.3, Mistral, and Code Llama for offline development, prototyping, and experimentation without cloud API costs. Ideal for privacy-sensitive applications and rapid iteration.

langchain.narrative: LangChain is a framework for building applications powered by language models. It provides tools for chaining LLM calls, implementing RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation), managing conversation memory, and orchestrating AI agents with tool usage capabilities.

huggingface.narrative: Hugging Face is the leading open-source platform for machine learning models and datasets. It hosts thousands of pre-trained models for NLP, computer vision, and audio processing. Essential for fine-tuning transformers, generating embeddings, and deploying custom ML models.

react.narrative: React is a JavaScript library for building component-based user interfaces. It uses a virtual DOM for efficient rendering, hooks for state management and side effects, and a unidirectional data flow. The foundation for modern web applications with a massive ecosystem.

nextjs.narrative: Next.js is a React framework for production-grade web applications. It offers server-side rendering (SSR), static site generation (SSG), incremental static regeneration (ISR), and the App Router with React Server Components for optimal performance and SEO.

typescript.narrative: TypeScript adds static typing to JavaScript, catching errors at compile time and improving developer experience. It supports generics, union types, interfaces, and advanced type inference. The standard for large-scale JavaScript applications.

javascript.narrative: JavaScript is the language of the web, running in browsers and on servers via Node.js. ES6+ features include arrow functions, destructuring, async/await, modules, and the Promise API. The foundation for both frontend and backend development.

nodejs.narrative: Node.js is a JavaScript runtime built on Chrome's V8 engine for server-side development. Its non-blocking, event-driven architecture excels at handling concurrent connections. Powered by npm, the world's largest package ecosystem.

nestjs.narrative: NestJS is a TypeScript-first framework for building scalable server-side applications. Inspired by Angular, it uses decorators, dependency injection, and a modular architecture. Supports REST, GraphQL, WebSockets, and microservice patterns out of the box.

fastapi.narrative: FastAPI is a modern Python framework for building high-performance APIs. It features automatic OpenAPI documentation, type validation with Pydantic, async/await support, and dependency injection. One of the fastest Python frameworks available.

flask.narrative: Flask is a lightweight Python micro-framework for web applications and APIs. Its minimalist design provides flexibility and control, making it ideal for small services, ML model serving, and rapid prototyping. Extensible through a rich plugin ecosystem.

python.narrative: Python is a versatile language dominating machine learning, data science, and backend development. Its ecosystem includes NumPy, Pandas, Scikit-learn, PyTorch, and TensorFlow. Known for readability, clean syntax, and the 'batteries included' philosophy.

django.narrative: Django is a high-level Python framework following the 'batteries included' philosophy. It provides an ORM, admin interface, authentication system, and URL routing out of the box. Ideal for rapid development of secure, database-driven web applications.

springboot.narrative: Spring Boot simplifies Java enterprise development with auto-configuration, embedded servers, and production-ready features. Part of the Spring ecosystem, it supports microservices, security, data access, and cloud-native applications at scale.

express.narrative: Express.js is a minimal and flexible Node.js web framework. It provides a thin layer of middleware for handling HTTP requests, routing, and building RESTful APIs. The most popular Node.js framework with a vast ecosystem of plugins.

postgresql.narrative: PostgreSQL is a powerful open-source relational database known for reliability and feature richness. It supports JSON/JSONB, full-text search, geospatial queries (PostGIS), CTEs, window functions, and advanced indexing strategies.

mongodb.narrative: MongoDB is a document-oriented NoSQL database storing data as flexible JSON-like documents. It excels at rapid prototyping, horizontal scaling with sharding, and handling unstructured data. Features aggregation pipelines and geospatial queries.

prisma.narrative: Prisma is a next-generation TypeScript ORM providing complete type safety from database to application. It includes Prisma Client for type-safe queries, Prisma Migrate for schema versioning, and Prisma Studio for visual database management.

chromadb.narrative: ChromaDB is an open-source vector database for AI applications. It stores embeddings for semantic search and RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) systems. Enables similarity searches, long-term memory for LLMs, and integration with LangChain.

redis.narrative: Redis is an in-memory data store used for caching, session management, real-time analytics, and message brokering. It supports data structures like strings, hashes, lists, sets, and sorted sets with sub-millisecond latency.

gcp.narrative: Google Cloud Platform offers scalable cloud infrastructure including Cloud Run for serverless containers, Cloud SQL for managed databases, BigQuery for analytics, and Vertex AI for machine learning. Trusted for high-availability production workloads.

azure.narrative: Microsoft Azure provides enterprise cloud services including compute, databases, AI, and DevOps. Features Azure Functions for serverless, Azure Active Directory for identity, and Azure Cognitive Services for AI. Deep integration with the Microsoft ecosystem.

git.narrative: Git is the industry-standard distributed version control system. It enables branching, merging, rebasing, and collaborative workflows like Git Flow and trunk-based development. Essential for team collaboration and code history management.

githubActions.narrative: GitHub Actions is a CI/CD platform integrated directly into GitHub repositories. It automates build, test, and deployment workflows with YAML-defined pipelines. Supports matrix builds, caching, secrets management, and thousands of community actions.

docker.narrative: Docker enables containerization for consistent development and production environments. Multi-stage builds optimize image size, docker-compose orchestrates multi-service stacks, and container registries distribute images. The standard for modern deployment.

vercel.narrative: Vercel is a deployment platform optimized for frontend frameworks, especially Next.js. It offers edge functions for minimal latency, preview deployments for pull requests, built-in analytics, and instant rollbacks. Zero-config deployment with exceptional DX.

jest.narrative: Jest is a JavaScript testing framework with zero-config setup. It provides built-in mocking, code coverage, snapshot testing, and parallel test execution. The standard for testing React applications, Node.js services, and TypeScript projects.

pytest.narrative: PyTest is the de facto Python testing framework with a clean, expressive syntax. It features powerful fixtures, test parametrization, async support, and a rich plugin ecosystem. Ideal for unit, integration, and end-to-end testing.

cypress.narrative: Cypress is a modern end-to-end testing framework for web applications. It runs tests directly in the browser with time-travel debugging, automatic waiting, and network interception. Provides fast, reliable tests with an excellent developer experience.
